channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Biden has authorized Ukraine to use long-range missiles inside Russia: Reports
item.description: President Biden authorized Ukraine to use U.S.-supplied missiles to strike further into Russia, multiple reports said. The move comes as about 10,000 North Korean troops aid Russia in its war and expect to fight in Ukraine in the “next several weeks.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: An Israeli strike in Beirut kills Hezbollah's spokesman, while a strike in Gaza kills at least 30
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A rare Israeli strike in central Beirut killed the Hezbollah militant group's chief spokesman on Sunday, while an Israeli strike in northern Gaza ’s Beit Lahiya killed at least 30 people, a hospital director there told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: FADI TAWIL, BASSEM MROUE and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bevy of immigration programs at risk under Trump mass deportation plan
item.description: Immigrants with humanitarian or temporary legal status in the United States are at risk of being rendered effectively undocumented by the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Tropical Storm Sara weakens to tropical depression after making landfall in Belize
item.description: POTRERILLOS, Honduras (AP) — Tropical Storm Sara on Sunday weakened to a tropical depression after making landfall in Belize, where forecasters expected heavy rain to cause flash flooding and mudslides.
item.dc:creator: MOISÉS CASTILLO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia launched more than 200 missiles, drones at Ukrainian infrastructure: Zelensky
item.description: On Sunday, Russia launched a drone and missile attack on Ukraine in what’s being seen as the largest barrage in recent months, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US-supplied long-range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: President Joe Biden has authorized the use of U.S.-supplied long-range missiles by Ukraine to strike even deeper inside Russia, the latest easing of limitations meant to prevent the conflict from further spiraling, according to one U.S. official and three people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: British prime minister says he has no plans to talk with Putin as he reaffirms support for Ukraine
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday that he has no plan to speak with Russian President Vladimir Putin as he pledged support for Ukraine as the U.K.’s top priority at this week’s G20 summit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia grinds deeper into Ukraine after 1,000 days of grueling war
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — When Russian tanks rolled into Ukraine in February 2022, the conventional wisdom was that the capital, Kyiv, would soon fall and the rest of the country wouldn’t last long against a much larger enemy. Instead, it was that narrative that quickly collapsed.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cruise line offers 4-year cruise to 'skip forward,' in wake of U.S. election results
item.description: One cruise line is offering travelers a customized journey for up to four years, to "skip forward," in the wake of the recent presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Protesters in separatist Georgian region occupy government buildings, calling for leader's ouster
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Opposition protesters in Georgia’s breakaway province of Abkhazia on Sunday refused to cede control of key government buildings seized during rallies earlier in the week during which at least 14 people were injured in clashes with police.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australian zoo asks citizens to catch deadly spiders
item.description: Despite the zany ask, there is a valiant reason behind the call from The Australian Reptile Park in Sydney, New South Wales.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Russia launches one of its fiercest missile and drone attacks at Ukraine's infrastructure
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Russia has launched 120 missiles and 90 drones in a large-scale attack across Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Latest typhoon lashes the Philippines, causing tidal surges and displacing massive numbers of people
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A powerful typhoon wrecked houses, caused towering tidal surges and forced hundreds of thousands of people to flee to emergency shelters as it cut across the northern Philippines on Sunday in the sixth major storm to hit the country in less than a month.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin critics lead a march in Berlin calling for democracy in Russia and an end to war in Ukraine
item.description: Prominent Russian opposition figures are leading a march of more than 1,000 people in central Berlin.
item.dc:creator: Markus Schreiber, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: VIDEO: Severe flight turbulence on Florida-bound flight sends person airborne, requires plane inspection
item.description: A Miami-bound flight turned frightening after the plane experienced severe turbulence, causing a diversion and requiring inspection of the plane.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy: Russia using Iranian weapons in 'massive' attack targeting energy infrastructure
item.description: Ukrainian forces say Russia launched over 200 drones and missiles against the nation's power and energy infrastructure this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senegal votes in election that will decide if president can carry out the reforms he promised
item.description: People in Senegal are voting in a parliamentary election that will decide whether the country’s president will be able to carry out ambitious reforms, six months after he was voted in on an anti-establishment platform.
item.dc:creator: Mark Banchereau, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Knife attack at a vocational school in eastern China leaves 8 dead and 17 injured
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A stabbing attack at a vocational school in the eastern Chinese city of Wuxi on Saturday left eight people dead and 17 others injured, local police said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From the Amazon rainforest, Biden declares nobody can reverse US progress on clean energy
item.description: Joe Biden witnessed the devastation of drought up close as the first sitting American president to set foot the Amazon rainforest.
item.dc:creator: Gabriela Sá Pessoa, Aamer Madhani And Colleen Long, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In a meeting with Biden, China's Xi cautions US to 'make the wise choice' to keep relations stable
item.description: As U.S. President Joe Biden and China’s leader Xi Jinping met for the last time, Xi cautioned that a stable relationship between the two countries was critical to the 'future and destiny of humanity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 10 newborn babies die in India after fire rips through hospital neonatal unit
item.description: Ten newborn babies were killed in northern India after a fire swept through a neonatal intensive care unit on Friday; 38 newborns were reportedly rescued from the ward.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man arrested for allegedly trying to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on flight
item.description: A South Korean man was arrested in Peru for allegedly attempting to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on a flight back to Korea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Lebanon considers cease-fire plan while Israel launches new strikes
item.description: Lebanon is considering a U.S.-backed cease-fire plan while Israel launched new strikes in the area south of Beirut. Senior Lebanon officials are reviewing the proposal and it is based on the U.N. Security Council resolution 1701, The Washington Post reported.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: The family of Israeli-American hostage pleads with Biden and Trump to bring hostages home
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Over the past two weeks, the political landscape around the negotiations for a cease-fire in Gaza have undergone a dramatic transformation.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In Milei's latest drastic move, Argentina is sole UN holdout voting 'no' to ending gender violence
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — The usual suspects abstained from voting on a seemingly uncontroversial United Nations resolution that denounced violence against women and girls on Thursday — Iran, Russia, North Korea.
item.dc:creator: ALMUDENA CALATRAVA and ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dutch coalition survives crisis over top official resignation decrying offensive comments
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The Dutch government dominated by hard-right leader Geert Wilders survived a government crisis that centered on the resignation of the finance state secretary over what she saw as denigrating comments on immigrants after Israeli fans were assaulted following a soccer ga
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT and AHMAD SEIR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic senators call for federal probe into Musk's contact with Russia
item.description: A pair of Democratic senators called for a federal investigation into Elon Musk’s involvement in SpaceX’s federal contracts following reports the tech billionaire has been in regular contact with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Pope asks Church of Rome to help address housing crisis ahead of Holy Year in 2025
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis on Friday called on the diocese of Rome to respond to a growing housing crisis in the Eternal City, wading into the debate about rising rental prices that has only grown more acute with Holy Year celebrations in 2025.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Welsh government advised to create dog-free zones to combat racism
item.description: A climate change group has submitted a suggestion to Welsh government officials to create dog-free zones in an effort to combat racism, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican introduces bill to revoke normal trade relations with China
item.description: The top Republican focused on U.S. competition with China introduced legislation on Thursday to end normal trade relations with Beijing. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Moo Deng, Thailand's baby pygmy hippo, gets official song in 4 languages
item.description: In case you can’t get enough of the little pygmy hippo Moo Deng from Thailand, there's now an official song featuring the internet's favorite baby animal — released in four languages for her global fans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's Rubio pick divides Republicans
item.description: Tensions are brewing over President-elect Trump’s move to tap Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.) to serve as secretary of State, with some of the president-elect’s allies voicing concerns about the pick. The choice has already set into motion the process of Florida Gov.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, The Hill

item.title: US warns Russia potentially aiding North Korea's nuclear program in direct threat to Europe, Asia
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken warns that North Korea is benefiting from aiding Russia in its war against Ukraine by potentially aiding Pyongyang's nuclear development, posing a threat for Europe and Asia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Church of England head Justin Welby resigns over handling of sex abuse scandal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Archbishop of Canterbury Justin Welby, head of the Church of England and spiritual leader of the global Anglican Communion, resigned Tuesday after an investigation found that he failed to tell police about serial physical and sexual abuse by a volunteer at Christian summer camps as soo
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation
